description: Term-to-anchor mapping for WEO methodology cross-reference system. Maps UI-visible terms to section anchors in the self-hosted V1.5 Methodology Manual.

mappings.context: Tier classification — verified cross-bloc adoption or implementation by the principal AI actor of each of at least two distinct ecosystems
Tier classification — structural separation between blocs through restriction, buildout, or regulatory barriers
Tier classification — coordination within a single bloc
Event classification by coordination scale — Cross-Bloc, Between-Bloc, Intra-Bloc
Bloc classification — US-anchored AI infrastructure ecosystem
Bloc classification — China-anchored AI infrastructure ecosystem
Bloc classification — nations with active partnerships with both blocs and strategic autonomy
Tier 2 bloc classification — fragmentation action initiated by Western Bloc
Tier 2 bloc classification — fragmentation action initiated by Eastern Bloc
Event type — multi-nation agreements, joint initiatives, standards cooperation
Event type — physical/digital systems deployment by government/sovereign entity
Event type — regulations, laws, executive orders, governance frameworks
Event type — private-sector entity action, unilateral or coordinated (partnerships, market structure, product deployment)
Domain classification — physical AI infrastructure, energy systems, data centres, 20–60 year durability
Domain classification — military, national security, defence applications
Domain classification — algorithms, models, software, standards, 2–5 year durability
Domain classification — economic exchange, investment, market access
CC confidence level — official document explicitly establishes connection
CC confidence level — authoritative statement documents connection
CC confidence level — analytical determination through convergent quality sources; sole confidence level for Parallel Policy, serving as both floor and ceiling (§9.3.2)
CC type — higher-authority event mandates implementation by lower-level event; documentary type at CC-V or CC-E (evidence route determines level)
CC type — multiple entities publish single unified document
CC type — financial resources from Event A directly enable Event B
CC type — Event A creates legal/regulatory basis making Event B possible
CC type — Event B is direct, deliberate reaction to Event A; temporal window set by the applicable Response Band (§9.4.1)
CC type — events formally part of same standards suite, regulatory family, or institutional framework; bidirectional (siblings) or parent-to-member where the parent framework is itself a WEO event
CC type — similar events in different jurisdictions without documented coordination; four-criterion assessment with Criterion 1 (Mechanism Similarity) as mandatory gate, CC-A as floor and ceiling
Documented relationship between verified events — seven types with three confidence levels
Environmental Nexus Tag — documented demand on environmental resources at hyperscale energy capacity
Environmental Nexus Tag — environmental objectives as stated justification in operative provisions
Environmental Nexus Tag — documented, measured environmental outcomes

Environmental Nexus Tag — authoritative environmental risk data documents a specific dependency; water-stress dependency requires the coupled two-condition gate (documented water-stress zone plus documented consumptive cooling/process stage)
Environmental Nexus Tag — binding environmental requirements on AI infrastructure or dedicated AI for environmental purposes
Five-type analytical enrichment layer identifying environmental intersections
ENT-1 count ÷ ENT-5 count within the event corpus — reported with component counts (n: ENT-1/ENT-5); provisional while the ENT-5 count is below 10
Actor designation — ecosystem anchor scoring ≥3 dimensions, passing severance test, holding sustainable platform dimension
Actor designation — structural chokepoint leverage without providing the ecosystem itself
Actor designation — ≥3 dimensions but failing severance test against a PAA
Actor designation — fewer than 3 dimensions met, operating within ecosystems shaped by PAAs and AIKs
Seven-dimension framework assessing sovereign AI infrastructure capability
Four-designation classification derived from three-gate structure
SCP dimension — designs and commercially markets AI training-relevant accelerators
SCP dimension — production-capable fabrication at ≤7nm
SCP dimension — globally essential semiconductor production equipment
SCP dimension — hyperscale cloud AI compute on actor's territory
SCP dimension — state-of-the-art foundation models with general applicability
SCP dimension — AI-relevant compliance frameworks with extraterritorial reach
SCP dimension — nationally transformative public capital towards AI infrastructure
SCP Gate 2 — ecosystem independence test against each existing PAA
PAA designation — capability threshold, severance test, sustainable platform dimension
T2 fragmentation type — parallel capability development reducing cross-bloc dependency
T2 fragmentation type — explicit restriction on other-bloc access
T2 Restriction label — export controls, sanctions with named other-bloc entities
T2 Restriction pathway — PIET Gate 3 byproduct establishing cross-bloc impact
Five-factor assessment for single-jurisdiction policy and institutional events
Multi-nation coordination events auto-qualify for geopolitical significance
Physical AI infrastructure significance — 2+ of 5 factors required
Software/platform infrastructure significance — four strategic routes
Geopolitical significance assessment gate
Stage 2 scope filter — Options A through G for demonstrating AI infrastructure connection
AI Connection pathway — ≥10% AI allocation or ≥$100M absolute
AI Connection pathway — ≥1 titled section about AI in official documents
AI Connection pathway — AI as primary purpose
AI Connection pathway — independent technical evidence for Infrastructure events

AI Connection pathway — official corporate communication characterising the transaction in Option A/B/C-equivalent terms, meeting transaction specificity, contemporaneity, and senior authorisation; Corporate events only
AI Connection pathway — published defence appropriation with explicit AI designation (Policy events)
AI Connection pathway — instrument controls documented AI infrastructure input material (Policy events)
Source accommodation — four-gate post-implementation evidence for policy restriction events
Source accommodation — operational evidence for Infrastructure events without AI-specific documentation
Source accommodation — state media with official documentary function under specified conditions
Versioned register of nation-to-bloc classifications with universal membership categories
Bloc membership — deepest alignment level in universal membership categories
Bloc membership — second-tier alignment in universal membership categories
Bloc membership — third-tier alignment in universal membership categories
Bloc membership — lightest alignment in universal membership categories
Event-level bloc attribution using three-priority hierarchy for Non-Aligned nation events
Parallel Policy scope assessment — status of scale comparison data availability
Two-level metric selection for Parallel Policy scope assessment
One-sentence document-verifiable description of how event provisions address an industry or environmental intersection
Documented evidence supporting a Basel 5-Factor assessment
Immutable original qualified values at time of initial database entry
System for incorporating post-entry developments with 6-month verification standard
Source-verified development awaiting 6-month temporal qualification
Framework for determining transformation (new event) vs scaling (rolling update)
Three-dimension diagnostic for corporate product releases (Dimension A/B/C)
PISA-5 Factor 1 — five-tier jurisdictional classification
PISA-5 accommodation for AI-significant jurisdictions below standard J-Tier thresholds
Option F and Option G events exempt from PISA-5 Factor 3 (AI Centrality)
PISA-5 marginal case resolution — Precedent-Setting, Resource Magnitude, Institutional Scope, Temporal Significance, Bloc Significance
Six-level hierarchy distinguishing Policy Cascade from Regulatory Enablement
Domain-specific temporal proximity thresholds for Types 1, 3, 4, and 7; Type 5 uses Response Bands; includes the Chinese 根据 48-month accommodation
Extended temporal window for Chinese 根据 regulatory chains
Funding Flow minimum — ≥10% of Event B's funding traceable to Event A
CC prerequisite — events must involve separate entities, assessed at entity level with the subsidiary clause and common-control extension; structural co-membership assessment governs Type 6
Industry tag category
Industry tag category
Industry tag category
Industry tag category
Industry tag category
Industry tag category
Industry tag category
Industry tag category
Industry tag category
Analytical enrichment layer identifying which industries event provisions address
Temporal requirement — events enter the database at least 6 months post-implementation; verified product releases enter from 60 days under §3.9.2
Primary → Corroborating → Supplementary → Excluded source classification
Tiered verification language — Confirmed, Established, Reported, Assessed, Claimed, Unconfirmed
Five stages across three phases — Qualification, Classification, Relationship Mapping
Stages 1–2 — Event Verification and Significance Assessment
Stages 3–4 plus Industry Tags and ENTs
Stage 5 — Coordination Connections cross-event analysis
ENT evidence basis — qualifying evidence appears in event's own source documentation
ENT evidence basis — analyst assessment based on authoritative third-party data
Domain-typical asset lifespan ranges used in classification
Observable policy adjustments through negotiation or mutual accommodation
Demonstrated Convergence — approximately one-third of relevant states adopt
Proposed → Signed → Ratified → In Force → Implemented → Verified
Four-pattern taxonomy for Chinese, Japanese, and Korean text in WEO
Adjusted currency ceilings during formally defined conflict conditions
Maximum permissible source age by data type
Evidentiary rigour required for different claim types — distinct from event classification tiers
Rolling update versioning — +0.1 (update), +1.0 (classification change), +0.0.1 (error correction)
Same-type CC reinforcement documented as entries in existing CC's verificationHistory
Bidirectional succession relationships between terminated and successor events
Nine conditions for automatic event exclusion
Chinese official computing infrastructure classification — general, intelligent, supercomputing
OEP operative designation establishing AI connection for Pathway C
SCP governing rule — physical infrastructure follows territorial sovereignty; corporate capability follows ownership chain
SCP rule — foreign-owned entities assessed for parent's territory unless strategic autonomy demonstrated
Bloc membership — hardware dimensions resist reclassification more than governance dimensions
Transitional classification mechanism when a new PAA qualifies under §5.3

variants: Financial Backing / Critical Technology / Strategic Leadership
mappings.context: Event-level bloc attribution for Non-Aligned nation events
Goods, services, and IP controlled by a PAA's sovereign jurisdiction or extraterritorial instruments
SCP evidence standard — canonical instruments, institutional datasets, analytical sources
AIK actor profile — nature of structural chokepoint leverage
AIK actor profile — entities providing chokepoint capability
Enhanced verification protocols for non-English language events

Chinese text markers informing CC classification — 根据 and 依照 are context-dependent between Policy Cascade and Regulatory Enablement (§9.2.1); other markers indicate Response, Framework Family, or Joint Issuance
Chinese national standards — GB = mandatory (test for T2); GB/T = voluntary (automatic T3)
Chinese governance pattern — State Council directive ('1') with ministry implementation plans ('N')
Chinese national technical committee for AI standards
Framework Family evidence — ISO/IEC normative references or Chinese equivalent
PIET status — event held pending assessment at 12-month post-implementation mark
PIET gate — >50% global or >90% bilateral export decline
PIET gate — >20% sustained price change for ≥3 months
PIET gate — ≥2 other-bloc government policy responses within 18 months

mappings.term: PIET Gate 4: Named Entity Commercial Disclosure
mappings.context: PIET gate — ≥1 named entity primary corporate disclosure citing restriction
T2 test — mandatory compliance language + documented enforcement mechanisms
Verification protocol before classifying major events as T3
When and how verified events are revised
Records of developments that did not materialise — CANCELLED, DELAYED, MODIFIED, UNCONFIRMED
Part B pathway — CISA SIE, DMA Gatekeeper, NIS2, DORA designation
Part B pathway — $100M auto or $50M+ with Route 3 support
Part B pathway — 2+ of 4 criteria (dependencies, market share, organisations, technical designation)
Part B pathway — AI model adoption and coordination impact
SCP severance test — mutual deterrence condition where denial is operationally self-destructive
SCP severance test — cascade analysis extends one level to primary capability-sustaining entities
ENT boundary — ENT-2 marks environmental intent; ENT-5 marks operationalisation into binding requirements
ENT-3 distinguishes acute operational consequences from cumulative lifecycle impacts
ENT-4 authoritative index — facility-level water stress classification
ENT-4 authoritative index — country-level climate vulnerability
ENT-4 authoritative index — natural catastrophe loss database
What the methodology explicitly cannot capture
Core principle — classification based on observable facts from official public documentation

Factual cross-event link between an event naming an AI infrastructure asset and the event recording that asset’s production, creation, or supply — a single material fact, carrying no relationship assessment and no confidence grade (Chapter 10)
Four-type taxonomy of material links — fabricated_at, deploys, trains_on, powered_by — each read as a plain source-to-target statement running from an asset’s origin to its deployment or use

Threads require documentary evidence of the specific material fact; binary status with no graded confidence — temporal proximity, thematic similarity, or a general relationship do not establish a thread

The asset must carry a distinctive designation in the source event’s pointed field — a model designation for chips (architecture-generation names only where no finer designation exists), a named facility, operator, or source for energy and fabrication; entity-level foundry designations qualify where the origin event records the relationship at entity level

Infrastructure Threads are actor-agnostic factual links outside Chapter 9 — no connection type, no CC-V/E/A grade, no criteria assessment; the Distinct Actor Requirement does not apply and a thread may link two events involving the same actor

Three-band temporal calibration for Type 5 Response CCs, organised by response evidence type: Band A up to 90 days; Band B 91 days to 18 months; Band C 18 to 36 months with a document-type justification requirement (§9.4.1)

variants: Reduced Verification Lag for Verified Product Releases
mappings.context: Verified product releases enter under a 60-day lag where four conditions are met — Part B Corporate product-release class, verified general availability, continuous availability across the period, and a maturity re-review record per §3.15.13; recorded as verificationLag: REDUCED in the genesis block

Pending-update record established at entry for every reduced-lag event, eligible at implementation date + 180 days; reviews availability history, source stability, and record accuracy at the point the standard §3.9 window would have closed

Mandatory qualifying gate for Parallel Policy — both events use the same policy tool category and share at least one documented sub-category characteristic (the mechanism specificity sub-test, recorded as specification_overlap); if unmet, no further similarity assessment is conducted

Parallel Policy events share at least one industry tag, primary or secondary, assessed through the Chapter 7 tagging system; any tag change triggers Criterion 2 re-verification on every incident connection

CC-A is the sole confidence level for Parallel Policy, serving as both floor and ceiling — documentary proof of an inter-event connection would satisfy a Type 1–6 definition instead; where domain-level coordination evidence exists, the §9.4.3 routing rule applies

Before classifying at Types 1–6, the analyst considers whether both events arose independently in response to common conditions; where independent adoption cannot be ruled out and no documentary evidence establishes the connection, the relationship routes to Type 7

Corporate product releases are market actions, not coordination mechanisms — outside CC assessment across all seven types; corporate instruments such as power purchase agreements, buildout programmes, and procurement contracts remain assessable

Five-condition accommodation for Policy events with unpublished instruments — implementation confirmed across three or more independent source categories including an accountability-bearing disclosure, simultaneously satisfying §3.1 verification and Stage 2 AI connection (§4.11)

Industry tag category — quantum computing hardware, quantum networking and communication infrastructure (QKD), cryogenic and quantum-specific supply chains, error correction ecosystems, QCaaS access platforms, and sovereign quantum capability programmes

Industry tag category, structural secondary only — documented pathways to terrestrial telecoms carrier operations (5G/6G networks, spectrum allocation, sovereign telecoms programmes); never assigned as primary

A member state’s qualifying entity may also credit a supranational actor’s dimension assessment where the supranational level holds direct enforceable jurisdiction over the entity’s relevant decisions; jurisdictional basis documented on both register entries (§5.3.2)

Maps each analytical layer to its unit of analysis and defining chapter — event layers (Chapters 3–8), event-pair layers (Chapters 9–10), actor layers (SCP, Bloc Membership Register); layers are independent analytical operations

Where the assessed event is not itself a facility, Basel Factors 1, 3, and 4 assess the object the event controls, funds, produces, or restricts, and Factors 2 and 5 assess the event’s cross-border effect; at least one passing factor must rest on entity-specific evidence (§4.4)

Option E qualification evidence is classified Confirmed (regulated disclosure explicitly linking the transaction, or the three-requirement test plus a corroborating signal) or Likely (three-requirement test without corroboration); recorded as confidenceTier in the event’s qualification object

Qualifying Option E events are classified Operational (documented as functional) or Announced (committed or under construction but not yet operational); recorded as status in the event’s qualification object
